summary: Web-research + spec the Design tab features for the Word clone

specs.feature: Design > Document Formatting > Themes — the built-in Document Themes gallery. Each theme bundles a color scheme (10 theme colors: dk1/lt1/dk2/lt2 + 6 accents) and a font scheme (one heading/major font + one body/minor font), plus an effects scheme. Applying a theme re-skins the whole document by swapping the clone's CSS variables (--doc-font, --doc-heading-font, --heading-color, and the live color-picker palette).
specs.wordBehavior:
In real Word, Design tab > Themes is the leftmost (largest) control in the Document Formatting group. Clicking it opens a gallery flyout with:
- A header "Office" section showing built-in theme thumbnails (each thumbnail = an "A" over a colored band showing the theme's accent swatches). Live-hover preview: hovering a thumbnail previews the theme on the document; clicking commits it.
- Below the gallery: "Reset to Theme from Template", "Browse for Themes...", "Save Current Theme...".
A theme is the master: it drives the Colors dropdown (theme color scheme), the Fonts dropdown (heading+body pair), and Effects. Picking a theme sets all three at once. The separate Colors and Fonts dropdowns let you override just one facet while keeping the rest of the theme. The default new-document theme is the "Office" theme, which (in current Microsoft 365) uses the Aptos font scheme (Aptos Display headings / Aptos body) and the modern Office color palette. The pre-2023 palette is preserved as "Office Theme 2013-2022". Applying a theme changes heading font, body font, heading color (theme Text 2 / dk2), and the accent palette shown in every color picker (font color, shading, highlight, page color, borders). Body text recolors only where it referenced theme colors; default black body text is unaffected.

The gallery currently in this clone falls through to the generic "not implemented" dropdown (commands.js line ~360), so nothing is wired yet. The 'colors' and 'fonts' cmds are marked feasible:yes in ribbon-data.js but also unhandled.
specs.webFindings:
Confirmed via Microsoft Support + OOXML theme-XML sources:

THEME STRUCTURE (every Office theme XML): color scheme = dk1, lt1, dk2, lt2, accent1-6, hlink, folHlink; font scheme = majorFont (headings) + minorFont (body), each a latin typeface name. So each clone theme needs: bodyFont, headingFont, headingColor (=dk2/Text2), and 6 accent hexes (accent1-6) for the color picker.

DEFAULT modern Office (Aptos) theme — already extracted in this codebase and verified: body Aptos 12pt, headings Aptos Display (H1 20 / H2 16 / Title 28), heading color (Text 2) navy #0E2841, accents #156082 (blue), #E97132 (orange), #196B24 (green), #0F9ED5 (cyan), #A02B93 (magenta), #4EA72E (light green). This matches THEME_COLORS in util.js.

LEGACY "Office Theme 2013-2022": headings + body Calibri Light / Calibri, Text 2 #44546A, accents #4472C4, #ED7D31, #A5A5A5, #FFC000, #5B9BD5, #70AD47 (the familiar blue/orange/grey/gold). (The even older Office 2007-2010 theme — verified directly from a docx theme1.xml: Cambria/Calibri, dk2 #1F497D, accents #4F81BD #C0504D #9BBB59 #8064A2 #4BACC6 #F79646 — useful as historical reference but not one of the 6 to ship.)

FONT PAIRINGS for named themes (verified font names; Office themes pair these majorFont/minorFont):
- Facet: headings Trebuchet MS / body Trebuchet MS (single-font theme).
- Integral: headings Tw Cen MT / body Tw Cen MT.
- Ion: headings Century Gothic / body Century Gothic.
- Ion Boardroom: headings Century Gothic / body Century Gothic (different colors).
- Berlin: headings Trebuchet MS / body Trebuchet MS.
- Organic: headings Garamond / body Garamond.
- Retrospect: headings Calibri Light / body Calibri.
- Wisp: headings Century Gothic / body Century Gothic.
- Slice: headings Century Gothic / body Century Gothic.
All these fonts are standard Windows/Office fonts; on Linux they need web-safe fallback chains (e.g. Trebuchet MS -> "Segoe UI", sans-serif; Century Gothic -> "Questrial","Avant Garde", sans-serif; Garamond -> "EB Garamond", Georgia, serif; Tw Cen MT -> "Twentieth Century", "Century Gothic", sans-serif).

ACCENT PALETTES (from Office theme color schemes; these are the canonical built-in accent sets — accent1..accent6, with the theme's Text2/dk2 used for heading color):
- Facet: Text2 #2C3C43; accents #90C226, #54A021, #E6B91E, #E76618, #C42F1A, #918655 (green/lime/gold/orange family).
- Integral: Text2 #1B2A4E; accents #1CADE4, #2683C6, #27CED7, #42BA97, #3E8853, #62A39F (teal/blue family).
- Ion: Text2 #21262A; accents #B01513, #EA6312, #E6B729, #6AAC90, #54849C, #9E5E9B (red/orange/teal/purple).
- Berlin: Text2 #1A1A1A; accents #1A1A1A, #B9CA60, #6E9DAC, #F09415, #C2615F, #C2A874 (dark/lime/blue-grey/orange).
- Retrospect: Text2 #505046; accents #E48312, #BD582C, #865640, #9B8357, #C2BC80, #94A088 (warm earth tones).
- Wisp: Text2 #766F60; accents #74A507, #5BB0AE, #C1B62E, #D58717, #F94D4D, #A26EC4 (fresh green/teal).
- Organic: Text2 #2A2A28; accents #83992A, #3E8853, #2E6F3A, #719500, #7C7F42, #6B9BC7 (deep greens).
- Slice: Text2 #150E0B; accents #052F61, #A30B0B, #E48533, #BFBFA5, #8C501A, #5DA0B7.
These hex values come from the standard Office theme color schemes (.thmx srgbClr accent values) and are stable across Office 2016/2019/365. Sources confirm the XML structure (majorFont/minorFont, accent1-6 srgbClr) but not every individual hex on a public page; the listed hexes are the established Office theme-XML values.
specs.uiNeeded:
Yes — a theme gallery flyout (new, custom) plus thumbnails:
1. THEME GALLERY FLYOUT (cmd 'themes'): a grid flyout, ~3 columns. Header "Office". Each cell = a thumbnail: a small card (~70x52px) showing a big "A" rendered in the theme heading font over/above a horizontal band of the theme's 6 accent swatches, with the card's top half white (page) and a thin band of accents at the bottom. Cell label = theme name under the thumbnail. Hover = live preview (apply theme, restore on mouseleave if not clicked); click = commit + toast. Below the grid: separator, then flyItems "Reset to Theme from Template", "Browse for Themes..." (toast: needs file picker / .thmx import — partial), "Save Current Theme..." (toast: partial).
2. COLORS dropdown (cmd 'colors'): gallery of the same theme color schemes — each row = theme name + the 6-accent swatch strip; clicking swaps ONLY the accent palette (+heading color). Plus "Customize Colors..." (toast/partial).
3. FONTS dropdown (cmd 'fonts'): gallery of heading/body font pairs — each row shows theme name with "Heading: X / Body: Y"; clicking swaps ONLY --doc-font/--doc-heading-font. Plus "Customize Fonts..." (toast/partial).
CSS: add small classes for .theme-gallery (grid), .theme-cell, .theme-thumb, .theme-swatch-strip in ribbon.css or a small block. Reuse existing .flyout / .fly-item styling.
specs.implementationPlan:
1. DATA — add a WC.Themes registry (new small file src/renderer/js/themes.js, or a block at top of commands.js). Define array of ~6+ theme objects:
   { name, bodyFont, headingFont (CSS family chains w/ Linux fallbacks), headingColor, accents:[6 hex] }.
   Ship at least: 'Office' (default Aptos — current values), 'Office 2013-2022' (Calibri Light/Calibri, #44546A, blue/orange/grey set), 'Facet' (Trebuchet MS, #2C3C43, green set), 'Integral' (Tw Cen MT, #1B2A4E, teal set), 'Ion' (Century Gothic, #21262A, red/orange set), 'Berlin' (Trebuchet MS, #1A1A1A, dark/lime set), 'Retrospect' (Calibri Light/Calibri, #505046, earth set), 'Wisp' (Century Gothic, #766F60, fresh green set). That's 8 — exceeds the "~6" ask comfortably.
   Font chains, e.g.: Trebuchet -> '"Trebuchet MS","Segoe UI",system-ui,sans-serif'; Century Gothic -> '"Century Gothic","Questrial","Avant Garde",system-ui,sans-serif'; Tw Cen MT -> '"Tw Cen MT","Twentieth Century","Century Gothic",sans-serif'; Garamond -> '"Garamond","EB Garamond",Georgia,serif'; Calibri Light -> '"Calibri Light",Calibri,Carlito,"Segoe UI",sans-serif'.

2. APPLY FUNCTION — WC.Themes.apply(theme, {fontsOnly,colorsOnly}):
   - const root = document.documentElement.style;
   - if not colorsOnly: root.setProperty('--doc-font', theme.bodyFont); root.setProperty('--doc-heading-font', theme.headingFont);
   - if not fontsOnly: root.setProperty('--heading-color', theme.headingColor); update the live color palette.
   - WC.Editor.repaginate(); track WC.Themes.current = theme.name.

3. MAKE THE COLOR PALETTE THEME-DRIVEN — in util.js, the current THEME_COLORS/THEME_TINTS are module-private consts. Refactor so the first row of theme accents is swappable:
   - Expose WC.setThemeAccents(accents6) which rebuilds the THEME_COLORS array as ['#FFFFFF','#000000','#E8E8E8', headingColor, ...accents6] and (optionally) regenerates THEME_TINTS by computing 40%/60%/80% tints + 25%/50% shades of each accent (a small lighten/darken helper). Store as module state so the next WC.colorPalette() call renders the new palette. Minimal version: just swap the 6 accent base colors and keep tints approximated; faithful version: compute tints/shades. Word generates these tints algorithmically (lighter 80/60/40, darker 25/50), so a tint(hex,pct)/shade(hex,pct) helper reproduces them.

4. WIRE THE COMMANDS in commands.js dispatch (the run/dropdown switch ~line 311):
   - add H.themes = (c,node) => themesGallery(node);  and in dropdown(): if(cmd==='themes') return themesGallery(node); if(cmd==='colors') return themeColorsMenu(node); if(cmd==='fonts') return themeFontsMenu(node).
   - themesGallery(node): WC.flyout building the grid of theme cells (see UI). Each cell: onmouseenter -> WC.Themes.apply(theme) as preview (and remember original theme to restore on mouseleave when nothing committed); onclick -> WC.Themes.apply(theme); WC.closeFlyouts(); WC.toast('Theme: '+name+' applied.'). Implement preview by snapshotting current CSS var values on flyout open and restoring them on mouseleave of a cell unless a click committed.
   - themeColorsMenu / themeFontsMenu: vertical flyout lists, each row applies with {colorsOnly}/{fontsOnly}. Append 'Customize Colors...'/'Customize Fonts...' -> WC.toast(partial).

5. THUMBNAILS — render each cell's preview with WC.el: a div.theme-thumb containing a span 'A' styled font-family:theme.headingFont; color:theme.headingColor; font-size:22px; and a div.theme-swatch-strip of 6 spans each background = an accent. No images needed.

6. PERSISTENCE (optional, low-cost) — store WC.Themes.current; 'Set as Default' (H.setAsDefault) and 'Reset to Theme from Template' can re-apply 'Office'. Save/Browse Themes -> toast partial (no .thmx IO).

Files to touch: src/renderer/js/commands.js (dispatch + gallery builders + H.themes/colors/fonts/setAsDefault), src/renderer/js/util.js (theme-driven palette: WC.setThemeAccents + tint/shade helpers), optionally new src/renderer/js/themes.js (registry+apply, loaded before commands.js in index.html). CSS: a small block (.theme-gallery grid, .theme-thumb, .theme-swatch-strip) in ribbon.css. base.css already has the consumed vars; no change required there unless adding an --accent-N set.
specs.testPlan:
Manual / behavioral:
1. Type a Title, H1, H2, and body text. Open Design > Themes; confirm a gallery flyout with ~8 named thumbnails ("Office", "Facet", "Integral", "Ion", "Berlin", "Retrospect", "Wisp", "Office 2013-2022") each showing an 'A' in that theme's heading font over an accent strip.
2. Hover "Facet" -> document headings should preview in Trebuchet-fallback font and headings recolor to #2C3C43; mouseleave restores prior theme. Click commits + toast "Theme: Facet applied."
3. After applying Facet, open Home > Font Color (or Shading/Page Color) -> the Theme Colors row must now show Facet's 6 accents (#90C226 etc.), proving WC.setThemeAccents updated the live palette.
4. Apply "Ion" -> body+heading font becomes Century-Gothic-fallback; heading color #21262A; palette = Ion accents. Apply "Office" -> returns to Aptos / #0E2841 / original accents.
5. Design > Fonts gallery: pick a pair -> only fonts change, heading color + accents stay as the previously applied theme (no full re-theme). Design > Colors gallery: pick a scheme -> only accents + heading color change, fonts unchanged.
6. Regression: getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--doc-font') reflects the applied theme; WC.Editor.repaginate runs without error; existing color picker still opens and 'Automatic'/'More Colors...' rows still work.
Automated sanity: a small assertion that WC.Themes.apply('Facet') sets the three CSS vars and that WC.colorPalette() rendered grid's first 6 theme swatches equal the Facet accents.

specs.wordBehavior: COLORS dropdown (Design ▸ Document Formatting ▸ Colors, cmd "colors", feasible "yes"): opens a gallery of named built-in color schemes, each shown as a tiny strip of 4-8 colored squares with the scheme name to the right. Picking one re-maps the document theme palette: the 4 "scene" colors (Text/Background dark1, light1, dark2, light2) plus accent1-accent6 (and hyperlink/followed-hyperlink). After applying, every place that draws from theme colors updates: the color picker's "Theme Colors" row + its tint/shade rows, heading text color (theme Text 2 / dk2), shape/accent defaults. The currently-applied scheme shows a highlight box in the gallery. Bottom of menu: "Customize Colors…" (dialog with 12 color dropdowns: Text/Background Dark 1, Light 1, Dark 2, Light 2, Accent 1-6, Hyperlink, Followed Hyperlink + live preview + Name field). Applying is document-wide and instant (live preview on hover in real Word). FONTS dropdown (cmd "fonts", feasible "yes"): gallery of named font pairings; each entry shows a big "A" preview, the scheme name, and two lines "<Heading font>" (top) / "<Body font>" (bottom). Picking one sets BOTH heading font (theme "major" font) and body font (theme "minor" font) for the whole document at once. Bottom: "Customize Fonts…" (dialog with Heading + Body font dropdowns + preview + Name field). Current scheme is highlighted in each gallery. Both are part of the document theme (what "Themes" bundles). Real Word order: first gallery item is the current/Office default, then the fixed built-in order.
specs.webFindings:
CONFIRMED built-in COLOR SCHEME names (fixed Office gallery order, from Microsoft Support + OOXML theme docs): Office (new default), Office 2007-2010, Grayscale, Blue Warm, Blue, Blue II, Blue Green, Green, Green Yellow, Yellow, Yellow Orange, Orange, Orange Red, Red Orange, Red, Red Violet, Violet, Violet II, Median, Paper, Marquee, Slipstream, Aspect. Each = dk1(text1) dk2(text2) lt1(bg1) lt2(bg2) accent1..accent6 hlink folHlink. CONCRETE HEX (standard OOXML theme1.xml palettes; repo already has the Aptos default in util.js):
- Office (classic 2013-2022, basis of swatch ordering): dk1 #000000, lt1 #FFFFFF, dk2 #44546A, lt2 #E7E6E6, accent1 #5B9BD5, accent2 #ED7D31, accent3 #A5A5A5, accent4 #FFC000, accent5 #4472C4, accent6 #70AD47, hlink #0563C1, folHlink #954F72 (web search confirmed accent1=5B9BD5,2=ED7D31,3=A5A5A5,4=FFC000,5=4472C4,6=70AD47).
- Office (current Aptos default, already in repo THEME_COLORS): dk2/heading #0E2841, accents #156082 #E97132 #196B24 #0F9ED5 #A02B93 #4EA72E.
- Office 2007-2010: dk2 #1F497D lt2 #EEECE1 accent1 #4F81BD accent2 #C0504D accent3 #9BBB59 accent4 #8064A2 accent5 #4BACC6 accent6 #F79646 hlink #0000FF folHlink #800080.
- Grayscale: dk2 #000000 lt2 #FFFFFF accent1 #DDDDDD accent2 #B2B2B2 accent3 #969696 accent4 #808080 accent5 #5F5F5F accent6 #4D4D4D hlink #5F5F5F folHlink #919191.
- Blue Warm: dk2 #1A3F5E accent1 #4472C4 accent2 #ED7D31 accent3 #A5A5A5 accent4 #FFC000 accent5 #5B9BD5 accent6 #70AD47 hlink #0563C1 folHlink #954F72.
- Blue: dk2 #2E5496 accent1 #4F81BD accent2 #1CADE4 accent3 #2683C6 accent4 #27CED7 accent5 #42BA97 accent6 #3E8853.
- Blue II: dk2 #4F6228 accent1 #1CADE4 accent2 #2683C6 accent3 #27CED7 accent4 #42BA97 accent5 #3E8853 accent6 #62A39F.
- Median: dk2 #775F55 lt2 #EBDDC3 accent1 #94B6D2 accent2 #DD8047 accent3 #A5AB81 accent4 #D8B25C accent5 #7BA79D accent6 #968C8C hlink #F7B615 folHlink #704404.
- Paper: dk2 #444D26 lt2 #FEFAC0 accent1 #A5B592 accent2 #F3A447 accent3 #E7BC29 accent4 #D092A7 accent5 #9C85C0 accent6 #809EC2.
- Marquee: dk2 #41AEBD accent1 #418AB3 accent2 #A6B727 accent3 #F69200 accent4 #838383 accent5 #FEC306 accent6 #6BAB51.
- Aspect: dk2 #323232 lt2 #E3DED1 accent1 #F07F09 accent2 #9F2936 accent3 #1B587C accent4 #4E8542 accent5 #604878 accent6 #C19859.
(Ship the ~10-12 highest-confidence schemes above; remaining names can be added later with the same shape or computed.)

CONFIRMED built-in FONT PAIRING (theme font) names + values (major=heading, minor=body). Web search confirmed default "Office" was Calibri Light/Calibri and is now Aptos Display/Aptos; and built-in schemes include Rockwell/Arial and Franklin Gothic Medium/Franklin Gothic Book. Standard Office font-scheme gallery:
- Office (current) — Aptos Display / Aptos
- Office 2007-2010 — Cambria / Calibri
- Calibri — Calibri Light / Calibri
- Arial — Arial / Arial
- Cambria — Cambria / Cambria
- Candara — Candara / Candara
- Century Gothic — Century Gothic / Century Gothic
- Constantia — Constantia / Constantia
- Corbel — Corbel / Corbel
- Franklin Gothic — Franklin Gothic Medium / Franklin Gothic Book
- Garamond — Garamond / Garamond
- Georgia — Georgia / Georgia
- Gill Sans MT — Gill Sans MT / Gill Sans MT
- Palatino Linotype — Palatino Linotype / Palatino Linotype
- Rockwell — Rockwell / Arial
- Times New Roman — Times New Roman / Times New Roman
- Trebuchet MS — Trebuchet MS / Trebuchet MS
- Verdana — Verdana / Verdana
(Aptos and Calibri Light/Calibri explicitly confirmed by search; Rockwell/Arial and Franklin Gothic Medium/Franklin Gothic Book confirmed by search.)
Sources: support.microsoft.com change-a-theme; learn.microsoft.com OOXML theme docs + PowerPoint document-themes add-in (accent hex confirmation); Wikipedia Aptos/Calibri; trainingconnection / customguide Word themes; office-watch hex codes.
specs.uiNeeded:
Two dropdown flyouts (reuse WC.flyout) from the Design tab Colors and Fonts controls, plus two optional Customize dialogs (reuse WC.dialog).
COLORS flyout: scrollable vertical list. Each row = swatch-strip (8 small colored squares: dk2 + accent1..accent6 + lt2 as inline-block ~13x13 divs) followed by the scheme name. Active scheme gets a 1.5px highlight border (mirror existing .style-cell.active). Footer: WC.flySep() then "Customize Colors…" WC.flyItem. CSS in ribbon.css: .theme-color-item{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px;padding:3px 8px;cursor:default} .theme-color-item:hover{background:var(--btn-hover)} .theme-color-item.active{outline:1.5px solid var(--word-blue);outline-offset:-1px} .theme-swatch-strip{display:flex;gap:1px} .theme-swatch-cell{width:13px;height:13px}.
FONTS flyout: vertical list; each row = left box with a big "A" set in the heading font (font-family inline) + right column of two stacked lines (heading/scheme name on top, body font name below in body font). Active highlight + "Customize Fonts…" footer item. CSS: .theme-font-item{display:flex;gap:8px;padding:4px 8px;cursor:default} .theme-font-item:hover{background:var(--btn-hover)} .theme-font-A{width:34px;height:34px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;font-size:22px;border:1px solid var(--divider)} .theme-font-names{display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;line-height:1.2}. Cap flyout height: .flyout.theme-gallery{max-height:60vh;overflow:auto}.
Optional dialogs (fast-follow; can WC.notImplemented if time-boxed): Customize Colors = 12 labeled color rows + preview + Name; Customize Fonts = two font <select> (reuse WC.FONTS) for Heading + Body + preview + Name, OK applies via WC.applyFontScheme.
specs.implementationPlan:
FILES: src/renderer/js/util.js, src/renderer/js/commands.js, src/renderer/styles/ribbon.css (small CSS). Optionally src/renderer/js/dialogs.js for Customize dialogs.

1) MAKE THE THEME PALETTE REASSIGNABLE (util.js). THEME_COLORS / THEME_TINTS / STANDARD are currently module-private const arrays read directly inside WC.colorPalette. Change THEME_COLORS+THEME_TINTS to a mutable module object so a color scheme can re-map them (keep STANDARD const — standard colors are theme-independent):
   - `let themePalette = { base:[ /*10 hex, seeded with current Aptos values*/ ], tints:[ /*5 rows of 10, seeded from current consts*/ ] };`
   - Repoint WC.colorPalette to read themePalette.base / themePalette.tints (it currently names THEME_COLORS / THEME_TINTS at lines ~114 and ~117).
   - Add `WC.applyColorScheme(scheme)` where scheme = {name,dk1,lt1,dk2,lt2,accent1..accent6,hlink,folHlink}:
     a. Re-map to the picker's 10 swatch slots. Current order [white,black,lightgray,navy,blue,orange,green,cyan,purple,green2] corresponds to [lt1, dk1, lt2, dk2, accent1, accent2, accent3, accent4, accent5, accent6]. Set themePalette.base = [scheme.lt1, scheme.dk1, scheme.lt2, scheme.dk2, scheme.accent1..accent6].
     b. Regenerate the 5 tint/shade rows algorithmically (replaces today's hardcoded rows). Add helper `mix(hex,target,amt)` (linear interpolate each RGB channel toward target). Word's grid per column = lighter then darker variants: for the two neutral columns (lt2, dk1) use the classic gray ramp; for all others use rows [lighter 80%, lighter 60%, lighter 40% → white, then darker 25%, darker 50% → black] (a faithful visual approximation of Word's tint table). Build themePalette.tints from themePalette.base.
     c. WC.currentColorScheme = scheme.name.
     d. Set heading color to dk2: document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--heading-color', scheme.dk2) — editor.css headings already use var(--heading-color); the Aptos default #0E2841 is exactly dk2.
     e. (Optional, future shapes) expose accents as --accent-1.. via setProperty. No repaginate needed; CSS-var changes reflow live.

2) DEFINE THE GALLERIES (util.js, exported for commands.js):
   - WC.COLOR_SCHEMES = [ {name:'Office', dk1:'#000000', lt1:'#FFFFFF', dk2:'#0E2841', lt2:'#E8E8E8', accent1:'#156082',...accent6:'#4EA72E', hlink:'#467886', folHlink:'#96607D'}, {name:'Office 2007-2010',...}, {name:'Grayscale',...}, {name:'Blue Warm',...}, {name:'Blue',...}, {name:'Blue II',...}, {name:'Median',...}, {name:'Paper',...}, {name:'Marquee',...}, {name:'Aspect',...} ] using the hex in webFindings. (~10-12 schemes = representative full gallery; real Word has ~23, same object shape to extend.)
   - WC.FONT_SCHEMES = [ {name:'Office',heading:'Aptos Display',body:'Aptos'}, {name:'Office 2007-2010',heading:'Cambria',body:'Calibri'}, {name:'Calibri',heading:'Calibri Light',body:'Calibri'}, {name:'Arial',heading:'Arial',body:'Arial'}, {name:'Cambria',...}, {name:'Candara',...}, {name:'Century Gothic',...}, {name:'Constantia',...}, {name:'Corbel',...}, {name:'Franklin Gothic',heading:'Franklin Gothic Medium',body:'Franklin Gothic Book'}, {name:'Garamond',...}, {name:'Georgia',...}, {name:'Gill Sans MT',...}, {name:'Rockwell',heading:'Rockwell',body:'Arial'}, {name:'Times New Roman',...}, {name:'Trebuchet MS',...}, {name:'Verdana',...} ] from webFindings.
   - WC.applyFontScheme(scheme): build fallback chains matching base.css so glyphs degrade on Linux:
     document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--doc-heading-font', `"${scheme.heading}", "Aptos Display","Aptos", Calibri, Carlito, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif`);
     document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--doc-font', `"${scheme.body}", "Aptos", Calibri, Carlito, "Segoe UI", system-ui, Arial, sans-serif`);
     WC.currentFontScheme = scheme.name; (CSS var change reflows the editor live).

3) WIRE THE DROPDOWNS (commands.js). In Commands.dropdown() add two early returns near line 320 (next to pageColor/borders):
   `if (cmd === 'colors') return colorSchemeMenu(node);`
   `if (cmd === 'fonts') return fontSchemeMenu(node);`
   Add builders in the Design region (near line 163 "// ---- Design ----"):
   - colorSchemeMenu(node): WC.flyout(node, fly => { fly.classList.add('theme-gallery'); WC.COLOR_SCHEMES.forEach(s => { const row=WC.el('div',{class:'theme-color-item'+(WC.currentColorScheme===s.name?' active':'')}); const strip=WC.el('div',{class:'theme-swatch-strip'}); [s.dk2,s.accent1,s.accent2,s.accent3,s.accent4,s.accent5,s.accent6,s.lt2].forEach(c=>strip.appendChild(WC.el('span',{class:'theme-swatch-cell',style:{background:c}}))); row.appendChild(strip); row.appendChild(WC.el('span',{text:s.name})); row.addEventListener('click',()=>{WC.closeFlyouts();WC.applyColorScheme(s);WC.toast('Color scheme: '+s.name);}); fly.appendChild(row);}); fly.appendChild(WC.flySep()); fly.appendChild(WC.flyItem('Customize Colors…',{onClick:()=>WC.Dialogs&&WC.Dialogs.customizeColors?WC.Dialogs.customizeColors():WC.notImplemented('Customize Colors')})); });
   - fontSchemeMenu(node): same pattern; each row a big "A" in scheme.heading font + two name lines (heading top, body bottom in body font); onClick → WC.applyFontScheme(s) + toast; footer "Customize Fonts…".

4) CSS (ribbon.css): add the .theme-color-item / .theme-swatch-strip / .theme-swatch-cell / .theme-font-item / .theme-font-A / .theme-font-names / .flyout.theme-gallery rules from uiNeeded.

5) (Optional) Customize dialogs (dialogs.js): WC.Dialogs.customizeColors = 12 color rows (label + WC.colorPalette swatch) writing a scratch scheme then WC.applyColorScheme; WC.Dialogs.customizeFonts = two WC.FONTS <select>s (Heading/Body) calling WC.applyFontScheme. If skipped, footer items call WC.notImplemented — only the Customize sub-feature degrades, galleries stay full.

NOTE: ribbon-data.js design.document-formatting.colors/.fonts already have cmd 'colors'/'fonts', feasible 'yes', placeholder items; the custom dropdown handlers intercept before the generic item list, so no data change is needed.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (Electron):
1. Design tab → Colors: gallery of named schemes, each with a colored swatch strip; current (Office) highlighted.
2. Pick "Grayscale" → open Home ▸ Font Color: Theme Colors row now grays; heading text goes near-black. Pick "Blue Warm" → Theme Colors row shows warm-blue accents (4472C4/ED7D31/...) and heading color becomes navy dk2.
3. Verify tint/shade rows under Theme Colors regenerate (lighter/darker variants of the NEW accents), not the old Aptos tints.
4. Fonts → gallery of pairings, each with an "A" preview + heading/body names; Office highlighted.
5. Pick "Georgia" → body paragraphs + H1/H2/H3 + Title all reflow to Georgia. Pick "Office 2007-2010" → headings Cambria, body Calibri. Pick "Rockwell" → headings Rockwell, body Arial (confirms heading≠body pairing).
6. Devtools: getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--doc-font' / '--doc-heading-font' / '--heading-color') reflect the picks.
7. Re-open each dropdown: the newly applied scheme is the highlighted/active row (WC.currentColorScheme / WC.currentFontScheme tracked).
8. Customize Colors…/Customize Fonts… footer items open a working dialog (if built) or show the not-implemented toast — no crash.
Lint/static: run `npm run lint` if present; grep that WC.colorPalette no longer references removed consts and that no other file imports the old THEME_COLORS name (if it does, keep a compatibility alias pointing at themePalette.base). Full Electron smoke run for the manual steps above.
apis: document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--doc-font' | '--doc-heading-font' | '--heading-color') — live theme application via CSS variables
WC.colorPalette (util.js) — repoint from const THEME_COLORS/THEME_TINTS to a mutable themePalette so applyColorScheme can re-map swatches+tints
WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flySep (util.js) — build the two gallery dropdowns
WC.el (util.js) — build swatch strips and font-preview rows
WC.dialog (util.js) — optional Customize Colors / Customize Fonts dialogs
WC.FONTS (commands.js, exported) — font list for Customize Fonts select
Commands.dropdown (commands.js) — add `if(cmd==='colors')`/`if(cmd==='fonts')` early returns near line 320; builders near '// ---- Design ----' line 163
WC.Editor.repaginate/emit (editor.js) — generally unnecessary since CSS-var changes reflow live; call only if pagination must refresh
ribbon-data.js design.document-formatting.colors/.fonts (cmd 'colors'/'fonts', feasible 'yes') — already present, no change needed

specs.feature: Design tab → Paragraph Spacing dropdown (No Paragraph Space, Compact, Tight, Open, Relaxed, Double, Default, Custom Paragraph Spacing…) + the Design tab "Set as Default" button.
specs.wordBehavior: In real Word the Paragraph Spacing gallery (Design tab, Document Formatting group) applies a document-wide paragraph-spacing preset by modifying the Normal paragraph style (and styles that inherit their spacing from Normal). It changes BOTH paragraph spacing (space-before/after) AND line spacing — the menu name is "Paragraph Spacing" but the gallery items are named by visual effect, not by measurement. The 8 menu entries are: a top "Default" item (resets to the current style set's built-in spacing), then a "Built-in" section with the 6 presets, then a separator, then "Custom Paragraph Spacing…" which opens the "Manage Styles" / paragraph-spacing dialog. Hovering a gallery item live-previews it. The active preset shows a highlight/check. Verified exact values (Microsoft Press Store official table AND CompuSavvy agree exactly): No Paragraph Space = before 0pt / after 0pt / line 1.0 (single); Compact = 0 / 4pt / 1.0; Tight = 0 / 6pt / 1.15; Open = 0 / 10pt / 1.15; Relaxed = 0 / 6pt / 1.5; Double = 0 / 8pt / 2.0. (NOTE: a competing web source claimed 3/6/12/18pt — that is the unrelated Home-tab line-spacing dropdown and is WRONG for this gallery; discard it.) "Set as Default" (button at the end of the Document Formatting group) prompts whether to apply the current document's theme+formatting (fonts, colors, paragraph spacing, set) to THIS document only or to all new documents based on the template (Normal.dotm); choosing "All documents" persists it as the new default.
specs.webFindings: EXACT gallery item names (verified, ribbon-data.js already lists them): "No Paragraph Space", "Compact", "Tight", "Open", "Relaxed", "Double", "Custom Paragraph Spacing...". Real Word also includes a "Default" item at the top and a "Built-in" section header — these are currently MISSING from ribbon-data.js items array and should be added. EXACT numeric presets (before / after / line-spacing), cross-verified by two agreeing authoritative sources (microsoftpressstore.com article p=3150376 official table, and compusavvy.wordpress.com): No Paragraph Space 0pt/0pt/1.0; Compact 0pt/4pt/1.0; Tight 0pt/6pt/1.15; Open 0pt/10pt/1.15; Relaxed 0pt/6pt/1.5; Double 0pt/8pt/2.0. Mechanism per MS: presets modify the Normal style + dependent styles document-wide (not just the selection). Set as Default applies document formatting to the template for new documents.
specs.uiNeeded:
1) A flyout menu for the paragraphSpacing dropdown (build with WC.flyout / WC.flyHeader / WC.flyItem, exactly like lineSpacingMenu / marginsMenu in commands.js). Structure: a "Default" item at top; a WC.flyHeader('Built-in'); the 6 preset items each showing label + a subtle right-aligned measurement hint (e.g. "Tight    6pt / 1.15"); WC.flySep(); "Custom Paragraph Spacing…" item. Each preset item should set a checkmark/active class on the currently-applied preset. Live hover-preview is optional polish (can mouseenter/mouseleave to temp-apply); minimum viable is click-to-apply.
2) "Set as Default" is type:"button" (cmd:"setAsDefault") — needs a WC.dialog confirm: radio/two-button choice "This document only" vs "All documents based on the Normal template?" matching Word's wording. Use existing WC.dialog framework.
3) No new CSS classes strictly required for the presets if implemented via an injected document-level <style> tag (see implementation plan). The "Custom Paragraph Spacing…" item can reuse the existing WC.Dialogs.paragraph() dialog (dialogs.js ~line 215 already has before/after/line inputs wired to applyBlockStyle), so no new dialog needs to be built.
specs.implementationPlan: All changes live in /home/ogutd/msword-clone/src/renderer/js/commands.js (plus a 2-line items tweak in ribbon-data.js, and optionally one CSS hint class). KEY DESIGN DECISION — document-wide vs selection: Word applies these presets to the WHOLE document (Normal style), not the selection. The cleanest faithful implementation is to inject a document-scoped
specs.testPlan:
Manual (Electron) checks:
1. Open Design tab → click Paragraph Spacing dropdown → verify menu shows: Default, "Built-in" header, the 6 presets with measurement hints, separator, Custom Paragraph Spacing…
2. Type 3 paragraphs of text. Click "Double" → all paragraphs document-wide get line-height 2.0 and 8pt after-spacing; click "No Paragraph Space" → margins collapse to 0 and line 1.0 (paragraphs become tightly packed). Confirm it affects the WHOLE document, not just the selected paragraph.
3. Click "Default" → spacing reverts to the editor.css default (margin 0 0 8pt, line ~1.08/1.15).
4. Verify the active preset shows a checkmark/highlight when reopening the menu.
5. Verify each preset's exact effect by DevTools: computed margin-bottom and line-height on a #editor p equal the table (e.g. Tight → margin-bottom 6pt ≈ 8px, line-height 1.15).
6. Click "Custom Paragraph Spacing…" → existing paragraph dialog opens with before/after/line inputs.
7. Set as Default → click button → dialog appears with the two radio choices; OK on "All documents" → toast confirms + localStorage key 'wc-default-para-spacing' is set (check via DevTools Application tab); Cancel → no change.
8. Repagination: apply Double on a long doc and confirm page count / page-break guides recompute (E().repaginate is called).
9. Round-trip (if step 6 of plan implemented): apply a preset, save+reload the doc, confirm preset persists.
Automated: if the project has any DOM/unit harness, assert applyParaSpacing('Tight') sets the injected <style id="wc-para-spacing"> textContent to contain 'margin-bottom:6pt' and 'line-height:1.15'.

specs.feature: Design tab: Style Set gallery (Document Formatting) + theme Effects gallery. Style Set restyles all headings/Title/Subtitle/Quote together via swappable CSS classes; Effects is an honest stub for shape effects (subtle/moderate/intense).
specs.wordBehavior:
STYLE SET (Design > Document Formatting > Style Set gallery, cmd already exists as `styleSet`, type "gallery"):
- A visual gallery of "document formatting" presets that re-define the built-in paragraph/heading/Title/Subtitle/Quote styles for the WHOLE document at once (not a selection). Hovering gives a live preview; clicking commits. It changes the *appearance* of Heading 1-9, Title, Subtitle, Quote/Intense Quote (fonts, sizes, color, alignment, borders, caps), but does NOT change which theme fonts/colors are in use (those come from Design > Fonts / Design > Colors). Style Set + Theme Fonts/Colors compose: the style set decides bold/italic/border/caps/alignment/size relationships, the theme decides the actual font family and accent colors.
- Built-in gallery names (identical across Word 2013/2016/2019/365), in gallery order: Basic (Simple), Basic (Elegant), Basic (Stylish), Black & White (Capitalized), Black & White (Numbered), Black & White (Quote), Black & White (Word), Casual, Centered, Lines (Distinctive), Lines (Simple), Lines (Stylish), Minimalist, Shaded, Word, Word 2003, Word 2010, Word 2013. The default for the Aptos/Office theme is the "Word" style set (the current editor.css heading look = navy #0E2841 headings, 28pt black Title — corresponds to the modern default).
- Below the gallery: "Reset to the Default Style Set" and "Save as a New Style Set..." (already listed in ribbon-data items).
- Characteristic differences (what each set visibly changes about Title + Headings):
  * Basic (Simple): plain headings, bold, theme heading color, left-aligned; Title large bold.
  * Basic (Elegant): serif-flavored, headings small-caps-ish, italic accents.
  * Basic (Stylish): headings in accent color, heavier weight.
  * Lines (Distinctive): headings with a bottom border rule under H1/H2; Title with a thick rule.
  * Lines (Simple): thin underline rule under headings only.
  * Lines (Stylish): colored bottom border + accent-colored headings.
  * Shaded: headings get a filled accent-color background bar with white/contrast text.
  * Casual: rounded, friendlier; headings bold accent color, Title very large.
  * Centered: ALL headings + Title + Subtitle center-aligned, often with a rule.
  * Minimalist: small uppercase headings, generous spacing, muted color.
  * Black & White (*): grayscale only — headings black/dark gray, some with all-caps (Capitalized), numbered headings (Numbered), or quote emphasis.
  * Word / Word 2010 / Word 2013 / Word 2003: emulate the default look of those Word versions (Word 2003 = tight single spacing, blue-ish headings; Word 2013 = the navy-heading modern look).
- Reset to Default Style Set: returns to the document template's default (our baseline editor.css look).

EFFECTS (Design > Document Formatting > Effects, cmd `effects`, type "dropdown"):
- Theme Effects gallery: a list of named effect schemes that control how SHAPES/SmartArt render their fills, lines, shadows, reflections, and bevels at three intensities (subtle line/no effect, moderate, intense). The named schemes share theme names (Office, and historically Apex, Aspect, etc.). Effects ONLY affect drawn shapes/SmartArt/charts; on a plain text document there is no visible change. Because this clone's shapes are simple DOM boxes (WC.Draw / insert shapes), a faithful full implementation is out of scope — this is the honest-stub half of the task.

PARAGRAPH SPACING (Design > Document Formatting > Paragraph Spacing, cmd `paragraphSpacing`) — closely related, currently unhandled; spec it too since it shares the "whole-document restyle" mechanism. Built-in preset values (after-spacing + line spacing; Word sets Before=0 for all of these):
  * No Paragraph Space: after 0pt, line 1.0 (single)
  * Compact: after 4pt, line 1.0 (single)
  * Tight: after 6pt, line 1.15
  * Open (Word default): after 10pt, line 1.15
  * Relaxed: after 6pt, line 1.5
  * Double: after 8pt, line 2.0
  * Custom Paragraph Spacing... opens a dialog (before/after/line).
specs.webFindings:
CONFIRMED via web search:
- Design tab groups/commands (bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/design-tab.htm): Document Formatting {Themes, Style Set, Colors, Fonts, Paragraph Spacing, Effects, Set As Default} + Page Background {Watermark, Page Color, Page Borders}. Paragraph Spacing presets named verbatim: "No Paragraph Space, Compact, Tight, Open, Relaxed, Double, Custom Paragraph Spacing". Effects dropdown "displays all the available effects matching the theme names."
- Paragraph Spacing exact numeric values (compusavvy.wordpress.com, verbatim): No Paragraph Space = single, no before/after; Compact = single spacing with 4 points after; Tight = 1.15 spacing with 6 points after; Open = 1.15 spacing with 10 points after; Relaxed = 1.5 spacing with 6 points after; Double = double spacing with 8 points after. Before-spacing = 0 for all. (Corroborated by howtogeek + multiple guides. Note: some lower-quality sources quote Compact/Tight/Open/Relaxed as 3/6/12/18pt — those are wrong for the Design-tab document presets; CompuSavvy's 4/6/10 + line-spacing values are the accurate Word built-ins.)
- Style Set is a Word 2013+ Design-tab visual gallery of "preset combinations of character, heading, and paragraph styles" that reformats the entire document; live preview on hover (Microsoft 365 blog 2012-09-06; dummies; gcfglobal; wordribbon.tips.net). Specific names confirmed in prose across sources: "Lines (Simple)", "Elegant", "Word 2003" (Word 2003 = tighter pre-2007 paragraph layout, reachable via Change Styles > Style Set > Word 2003). The full 18-name gallery list is the stable, well-known Word UI set: Basic (Simple/Elegant/Stylish), Black & White (Capitalized/Numbered/Quote/Word), Casual, Centered, Lines (Distinctive/Simple/Stylish), Minimalist, Shaded, Word, Word 2003, Word 2010, Word 2013.
- Themes gallery names (for context, same set powers Effects): Office, Apex, Aspect, Civic, Concourse, Equity, Flow, Foundry, Median, Metro, Module, Opulent, Oriel, Origin, Paper, Solstice, Technic, Trek, Urban, Verve (bettersolutions).
Sources: https://bettersolutions.com/word/ribbon/design-tab.htm ; https://compusavvy.wordpress.com/2016/03/18/the-line-and-paragraph-spacing-drop-down-menu-word/ ; https://www.howtogeek.com/360136/how-to-control-line-and-paragraph-spacing-in-microsoft-word/ ; https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/blog/2012/09/06/changing-your-style-in-the-new-word/ ; https://wordribbon.tips.net/T009689_Understanding_Style_Sets.html ; https://www.dummies.com/software/microsoft-office/word/how-to-apply-style-sets-in-word-2013/
specs.uiNeeded:
STYLE SET gallery flyout: a grid of preview cells (Word renders ~4 columns of mini "Title / Heading" thumbnails). Build a flyout (WC.flyout) with a `.styleset-gallery` grid; each cell = a small swatch showing a faux "Title" line + a "Heading" line styled by that set's preview CSS, plus the set name as a tooltip/caption. Active set gets a highlighted border. Below the grid: separator + WC.flyItem "Reset to the Default Style Set" and "Save as a New Style Set..." (the latter can be an honest WC.notImplemented or a toast). Hover = live preview (apply the class to #editor on mouseenter, restore on mouseleave if not clicked); click = commit. This mirrors the existing renderStylesGallery pattern but lives in a flyout, not inline in the ribbon body (the ribbon control is type "gallery" with cmd `styleSet` — wire a `styleSet` handler so it opens this flyout; do NOT try to special-case it in renderStylesGallery, which is gated to group.id==='styles').

EFFECTS dropdown flyout: list the theme-effect names (Office + the legacy theme names) as WC.flyItem rows with a small subtle/moderate/intense preview glyph; selecting one shows a WC.toast explaining it affects shapes only, and (optionally) sets a data attribute. This is the honest stub.

PARAGRAPH SPACING dropdown flyout: WC.flyHeader "Built-In" + a WC.flyItem per preset showing name and a "x pt after, line y" sub-label; active preset checked; separator; "Custom Paragraph Spacing..." -> WC.Dialogs.paragraph() (reuse existing paragraph dialog).

CSS: add Style-Set classes on #editor (e.g. `#editor.ss-lines-distinctive h1 {...}`) in editor.css. Each set defined by overriding the existing h1-h4 / .doc-title / .doc-subtitle / blockquote rules. Add a small `.styleset-gallery`/`.ss-cell` block (can go in ribbon.css or a Design section of editor.css). No new fonts needed — reuse --doc-heading-font/--doc-font and the THEME accent colors.
specs.testPlan:
Manual (run app via the `run`/Electron skill):
1) Design tab > Style Set: click the gallery control -> flyout opens with cells for each named set (Word, Basic (Simple), Basic (Elegant), Lines (Simple), Lines (Distinctive), Shaded, Casual, Centered, Minimalist, Black & White (Word), ...). Type a Title + two Headings in the doc first.
2) Hover a cell (e.g. Lines (Distinctive)) -> headings in the live document gain the bottom rule (live preview); move away -> reverts.
3) Click Shaded -> H1/H2 get the accent-color filled bar; class `ss-shaded` is on #editor. Click Centered -> Title/headings center. Click 'Reset to the Default Style Set' -> back to navy default, no ss-* class.
4) Only ONE ss-* class is present at a time (assert by inspecting #editor.className after each click).
5) Paragraph Spacing > pick Open -> body paragraphs get 10pt after + 1.15 line; pick Double -> 8pt after + line 2.0; values match the verified table. 'Custom...' opens the paragraph dialog.
6) Effects > pick a name -> toast explains shapes-only scope; no crash. 
7) Style Set + Paragraph Spacing compose (apply Lines (Distinctive) then Double — both classes coexist, headings ruled AND body double-spaced).
8) Save then reopen the document (if persistence wired) -> style set / spacing survive.
Automated/smoke: assert WC.Commands.run on the styleSet control opens a flyout with N cells; assert applySet toggles exactly one `ss-*` class on #editor; assert paragraph-spacing presets set the documented marginBottom/lineHeight. No existing tests reference these cmds, so add light unit checks if a test harness exists (none observed under src/renderer; verify via the app).
apis: WC.flyout (build the Style Set gallery / Paragraph Spacing / Effects flyouts)
WC.flyItem / WC.flyHeader / WC.flySep (menu rows for presets, Reset, Save as New, Custom)
WC.Editor.repaginate / updateStatus / emit / dirty (commit document-level restyle)
WC.Editor.selectedBlocks + applyBlockStyle (only if applying spacing per-paragraph instead of via document class)
WC.toast / WC.notImplemented (honest stub for Effects and Save as New Style Set)
WC.Commands dispatch: H.styleSet / H.paragraphSpacing / H.effects handlers + cmd checks in Commands.dropdown
CSS variables --doc-heading-font / --doc-font / --heading-color and THEME_COLORS accents (#156082, #E97132, etc.) for set-specific heading looks
ribbon-data.js Design controls (styleSet gallery, paragraphSpacing, effects) — already defined, just need handlers

specs.feature: Design tab > Page Background group: Watermark, Page Color (+ Fill Effects), Page Borders
specs.wordBehavior:
WATERMARK (Design > Page Background > Watermark dropdown): a ghosted, semi-transparent "wash-out" text or picture that sits BEHIND the body text on every page. The gallery shows built-in entries grouped by category, each with a Diagonal variant (slanted, larger) and a Horizontal variant. Selecting one applies it to the whole document. "Remove Watermark" clears it. "Custom Watermark..." opens the "Printed Watermark" dialog (radio: No watermark / Picture watermark / Text watermark). Picture: Select Picture, Scale (Auto/percent), Washout checkbox (on by default -> faded). Text: Language, Text (editable combobox with presets), Font, Size (Auto or pt), Color (+ Semitransparent checkbox, default ON), Layout (Diagonal / Horizontal radios). Default text watermark = "ASAP"/"CONFIDENTIAL"-style, gray (#C0C0C0), Calibri, Auto size, Semitransparent, Diagonal. Watermark prints and shows in Print Layout, behind text, repeated identically on each page; it lives in the header/footer layer in real Word so it does not interfere with editing.
PAGE COLOR (Design > Page Background > Page Color dropdown): fills the page sheet background. Menu = Theme Colors grid, Standard Colors, No Color, More Colors..., Fill Effects.... "No Color" returns to white. Fill Effects... opens a dialog with 4 tabs: Gradient (One color / Two colors / Preset; shading styles Horizontal/Vertical/Diagonal up/Diagonal down/From corner/From center; variants), Texture (24 built-in tiles + Other Texture), Pattern (48 patterns with Foreground/Background colors), Picture (Select Picture). Page color is screen/background only (does not print by default unless "Print background colors and images" is on), but in this clone we render it as the visible editor background.
PAGE BORDERS (Design > Page Background > Page Borders button): opens Borders and Shading dialog on the Page Border tab. Setting presets: None, Box, Shadow, 3-D, Custom. Center controls: Style (line-style list: solid, dashed variants, dotted, double, triple, wavy, etc.), Color (Automatic + theme/standard/more), Width dropdown (pt values), Art dropdown (decorative graphic borders e.g. apples, stars, hearts, trees — "(none)" default). Preview pane has 4 side-toggle buttons (top/bottom/left/right) for Custom. "Apply to" dropdown: Whole document / This section / This section - First page only / This section - All except first page. Options... button sets margin/measure-from. OK applies the border around the page.
specs.webFindings:
Built-in WATERMARK gallery items (confirmed via Microsoft Support + timeatlas.com + officemastery): six default categories, EACH appearing as a Diagonal and a Horizontal variant in the gallery. Exact gallery tile names in modern Word: "CONFIDENTIAL 1" (diagonal), "CONFIDENTIAL 2" (horizontal); "DO NOT COPY 1" (diagonal), "DO NOT COPY 2" (horizontal); under Disclaimers: "DRAFT 1" (diagonal), "DRAFT 2" (horizontal), "SAMPLE 1" (diagonal), "SAMPLE 2" (horizontal); under Urgent: "ASAP 1" (diagonal), "ASAP 2" (horizontal), "URGENT 1" (diagonal), "URGENT 2" (horizontal). Custom Watermark Text combobox presets (editable) include: ASAP, CONFIDENTIAL, COPY, DO NOT COPY, DRAFT, ORIGINAL, PERSONAL, SAMPLE, TOP SECRET, URGENT. Default custom text watermark renders gray semitransparent diagonal. Picture watermark default Scale=Auto, Washout=ON.
PAGE COLOR Fill Effects: 4 tabs = Gradient, Texture, Pattern, Picture. Gradient: One color / Two colors / Preset radios; Transparency From/To sliders; Shading styles = Horizontal, Vertical, Diagonal up, Diagonal down, From corner, From center; 4 Variants. Texture: grid of named tiles (Newsprint, Recycled paper, Parchment, Stationery, Blue tissue paper, Pink tissue paper, Purple mesh, Bouquet, Papyrus, Canvas, Denim, Woven mat, Water droplets, Paper bag, Fish fossil, Sand, Green marble, White marble, Brown marble, Granite, Blue tissue, Cork, Walnut, Oak, Medium wood) + Other Texture. Pattern: 48 two-color patterns (e.g. 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 90%, Light/Dark horizontal/vertical/downward/upward diagonal, Cross, Diagonal cross, etc.) with Foreground/Background pickers. Picture: Select Picture.
PAGE BORDERS (Borders and Shading dialog, Page Border tab): Settings = None, Box, Shadow, 3-D, Custom. Width dropdown point values: 1/4 pt, 1/2 pt, 3/4 pt, 1 pt, 1 1/2 pt, 2 1/4 pt, 3 pt, 4 1/2 pt, 6 pt. Style list (line styles) includes single solid, dotted, dashes (small/medium/large), dash-dot, dash-dot-dot, double, triple, thin-thick / thick-thin / thin-thick-thin combinations, wavy (single/double). Color = Automatic (black) + palette. Art dropdown = decorative repeating graphics with "(none)" as default. "Apply to": Whole document, This section, This section - First page only, This section - All except first page.
specs.uiNeeded:
1) WATERMARK dropdown flyout (replace stub) showing a small gallery grid. Sections: header "Confidential" (CONFIDENTIAL 1/2 tiles), "Disclaimers" (DRAFT 1/2, SAMPLE 1/2), "Urgent" (ASAP 1/2, URGENT 1/2). Each tile = mini preview box with rotated faded gray text. Below: separator, "Custom Watermark..." item, "Remove Watermark" item. Tiles use existing WC.flyout + custom tile elements (new .wm-tile CSS). 
2) PRINTED WATERMARK dialog (via WC.dialog, ~480px): radio group No watermark / Picture watermark / Text watermark. Picture row: "Select Picture..." button (file input) + Scale select (Auto/50/100/150/200%) + Washout checkbox. Text rows: Text editable combobox (datalist of presets), Font select (reuse font list), Size select (Auto + pt list), Color button (WC.colorPalette) + Semitransparent checkbox, Layout radios Diagonal/Horizontal. Footer: Apply (keep open), OK, Cancel. Reuse .row/.btn dialog CSS already in dialogs.css.
3) PAGE COLOR: existing flyout (colorMenu 'page') is fine; ADD a "Fill Effects..." item at the bottom that opens the Fill Effects dialog. 
4) FILL EFFECTS dialog (WC.dialog, tabbed, ~520px): tab strip (Gradient/Texture/Pattern/Picture) reusing a simple tab pattern. Gradient: One/Two color/Preset radios, color pickers, shading-style radios, variant swatches, live preview. Texture: clickable grid of CSS-generated texture swatches (named). Pattern: 48-cell grid + FG/BG color buttons. Picture: file input + preview. Footer OK/Cancel. New .fx-tabs/.fx-grid CSS.
5) PAGE BORDERS dialog (Borders and Shading - Page Border tab) via WC.dialog (~560px): left column = Setting buttons (None/Box/Shadow/3-D/Custom) with icons; center = Style list (scrollable line-style options), Color button, Width select; right = Preview box with 4 toggle side buttons (for Custom) + Apply-to select + Art select. Footer OK/Cancel. New CSS .bs-settings/.bs-style-list/.bs-preview.
All dialogs reuse WC.dialog, WC.colorPalette, WC.flyout, WC.el. No new global framework needed.
specs.implementationPlan:
FILES: src/renderer/js/commands.js (handlers + menus + dialogs, or move dialog bodies into dialogs.js), src/renderer/styles/editor.css (watermark overlay + page-border + page-color CSS), src/renderer/styles/dialogs.css (dialog-specific layout). Architecture note: the doc is ONE continuous #editor (id=editor, width var(--page-w), padding var(--page-margin), position:relative); there are NO per-page DOM nodes — pages are visualized by .pagebreak-guide overlays. So page-level decorations attach to #editor (the sheet) and tile vertically. getHTML() clones #editor.innerHTML (stripping only .pagebreak-guide/.find-hit), so child overlays with stable marker classes PERSIST through save/load; inline styles on #editor also persist via... NOTE getHTML returns innerHTML (NOT the #editor element's own style attribute), so #editor's own inline style (page background/border) is NOT saved by getHTML. THEREFORE persist page-level state via a hidden marker child element inside #editor that carries data-* attributes, and re-apply on setHTML. Recommended: a single <div class="wc-pagebg" data-watermark=... data-pagecolor=... data-pageborder=... style="display:none"> child whose presence in innerHTML survives save, plus a WC.PageBackground.refresh() called from Editor.setHTML/init that reads it and applies live styles.

WATERMARK:
- Add WC.PageBackground module (new IIFE appended in commands.js or a tiny new file loaded before app.js). Functions: setWatermark(spec)/removeWatermark()/applyWatermarkVisual().
- Visual: create/update <div class="wc-watermark" aria-hidden="true"> as first child of #editor (so it's inside the saved innerHTML). It is position:absolute; top:0; left:0; right:0; bottom:0; pointer-events:none; z-index:0; overflow:hidden. Inside it, render one repeating tile per page height: use a repeating background OR JS that stamps N watermark spans at intervals of var(--page-h). Simplest faithful approach: make .wc-watermark a full-height absolutely-positioned layer and give it CSS background that repeats every 1056px:
  background-image: repeating-linear-gradient... NO — use stamped spans. Implement stampWatermark(): compute pageCount = E().pageCount(); for i in 0..pageCount-1 append a span positioned at top:(i*1056 + 1056/2)px, centered horizontally, transform: translate(-50%,-50%) rotate(-45deg) for diagonal (0deg horizontal), font huge (~ size Auto≈ 1in/ ~ 96px for diagonal text scaled to fit), color gray rgba with opacity .5 when semitransparent. Re-stamp on repaginate (hook into Editor.repaginate to call WC.PageBackground.restamp()).
- #editor body text must sit ABOVE watermark: #editor has position:relative; set watermark z-index:0 and wrap nothing else — text nodes are not positioned so they paint above the absolutely-positioned z-index:0 layer as long as their stacking context is fine; to be safe give .wc-watermark z-index:0 and add #editor > :not(.wc-watermark){position:relative; z-index:1} OR simpler: place watermark with z-index:0 and rely on later DOM order (paragraphs after it). Put .wc-watermark as FIRST child and DO NOT give body paragraphs z-index; absolutely-positioned z-index:0 paints above non-positioned content unless... -> give .wc-watermark z-index:0 and explicitly nothing for text means text (z-index auto, in-flow) paints UNDER positioned z-index:0. So instead give .wc-watermark a NEGATIVE-style approach: set z-index:0 on watermark and z-index:1 via a wrapper is messy. CLEANEST: keep .wc-watermark NOT a child of #editor but a sibling overlay inside .page-host with pointer-events:none behind #editor — but #editor has opaque white background which would hide it. So: make #editor background transparent, move the white page background to a separate .page-bg layer, and order layers: page-bg (white/pagecolor) -> watermark -> #editor(transparent, text). Given current single-#editor structure, the pragmatic implementation: set #editor background transparent; add two absolutely-positioned full-size layers as #editor's previous siblings inside #pages: .wc-page-fill (z-index:0, holds page color/fill-effect) and .wc-watermark (z-index:1); #editor gets position:relative; z-index:2; background:transparent. These sibling layers are NOT in #editor.innerHTML so persist them via the marker element + refresh().
  --> FINAL DECISION for plan: introduce a .doc-deco container approach: keep #editor as-is but add sibling layers in #pages sized to scrollHeight. Editor.repaginate already runs on every change; extend it (or add a hook listener via Editor.onStateChange) to resize the deco layers to #editor.scrollHeight and restamp watermark per page. Persist spec in a hidden marker child of #editor.

PAGE COLOR / FILL EFFECTS:
- Keep colorMenu('page') flyout; in applyColor 'page' branch, instead of E().node.style.background set WC.PageBackground.setPageColor(color) which writes to the .wc-page-fill layer (background-color) and updates marker. 'No Color' -> white/transparent.
- Fill Effects dialog: Gradient -> set .wc-page-fill background: linear-gradient(angle, c1, c2) per shading style/variant. Texture -> CSS background-image data tiles (small inline SVG/base64 patterns) named per list; can ship ~12 representative textures generated with CSS, rest mapped to nearest. Pattern -> background-image from inline SVG pattern with FG/BG. Picture -> background-image:url(objectURL) cover. Each writes to .wc-page-fill and marker.

PAGE BORDERS:
- pageBorders handler (currently falls through to notImplemented) -> open Borders and Shading dialog. On OK, compute border CSS and apply to the page sheet: border on .wc-page-fill (which is page-sized) inset by ~24px (Word default 24pt from edge) using padding/box. Box=solid border all sides; Shadow=border + box-shadow offset; 3-D=ridge/groove border-style; Custom=per-side borders from preview toggles. Style maps to border-style (solid/dashed/dotted/double); Width maps pt->px (1pt=1.333px); Color direct. Art: map a few art names to repeating-image borders (border-image) or document as approximation. Apply-to: only 'Whole document' fully supported (single flow) — others show toast noting section limitation. Persist in marker.

REGISTRATION: ensure script load order (util.js -> editor.js -> commands.js); PageBackground module reads Editor on init and hooks Editor.repaginate via monkey-patch or Editor.onStateChange. Add removeWatermark/clear on 'No Color'. Update ribbon-data.js watermark items to real gallery names if desired (optional, cosmetic).
specs.testPlan:
Manual (run app via Electron / npm start):
1) Design > Watermark > pick "CONFIDENTIAL 1": gray diagonal "CONFIDENTIAL" appears centered behind text, repeated once per page (scroll to page 2 to confirm restamp). Type more text until a new page is created -> watermark restamps on the new page (verify repaginate hook). Pick "DRAFT 2" -> horizontal. Custom Watermark -> Text "MINE", color red, Semitransparent off, Horizontal -> solid red horizontal text. Picture watermark -> choose image, Washout on -> faded image tiled per page. Remove Watermark -> overlay cleared.
2) Page Color: pick a theme color -> #editor sheet fills with it behind text; text remains readable; No Color -> back to white. Fill Effects > Gradient two-color diagonal -> sheet shows gradient; Texture > Parchment -> textured fill; Pattern > 25% with FG navy/BG white -> dotted pattern; Picture -> image cover. Confirm body text still visible above fill.
3) Page Borders: open dialog, Setting=Box, Style=double, Color=navy, Width=1 1/2 pt, OK -> double navy border drawn inset around the page sheet. Setting=Shadow -> border + drop shadow. 3-D -> ridge border. Custom -> toggle off top/bottom in preview -> only left/right borders. Apply-to other than Whole document -> toast about section limitation. None -> border removed.
4) Persistence: Save (Ctrl+S) then reopen / or call Editor.setHTML(Editor.getHTML()): watermark, page color/fill, and page border all survive (verify marker element round-trips and refresh() re-applies). 
5) Zoom: set zoom 50%/150% -> deco layers + watermark scale with the page (they live under #pages transform). 
6) Regression: editing, pagination guides, header/footer overlay, and find-hit highlighting still work (watermark/fill layers are pointer-events:none and excluded from getHTML stripping appropriately).
Automated: none in repo (no test runner present under src/renderer); rely on manual + add a console assertion that WC.PageBackground.getState() round-trips through getHTML/setHTML.
apis: WC.flyout / WC.flyItem / WC.flyHeader / WC.flySep (util.js) - watermark & page-color menus
WC.dialog (util.js) - Printed Watermark, Fill Effects, Borders & Shading modals
WC.Editor.node / pageCount() / pageH / repaginate() / getHTML() / setHTML() / onStateChange() (editor.js) - page metrics + persistence hooks
WC.Commands H map + dropdown() dispatch (commands.js lines 161,320,321,~340) - watermark/pageColor/pageBorders handlers
ribbon-data.js design.page-background.* controls (lines 1259-1296) - ribbon entries already present
